nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bohum
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Asmund, Iver, Syvert.
Norwegian (Bøyum): habitational name from a farm in Sogn, named from Old Norse bœr, býr ‘farm’.

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

en.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Bohum was born in 1304, in Europe. She had at least 4 sons and 3 daughters with Sir Ralph de Bromwich. She died in 1363, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Bohum est née en 1304, à Europe. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles avec Sir Ralph de Bromwich. Elle est décédée en 1363, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Bohum wurde 1304 in Europa geboren. Sie hatte mit Sir Ralph de Bromwich mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb 1363 im Alter von 59 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Bohum è nata il nel 1304 nel Europa. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie con Sir Ralph de Bromwich. Morì nel 1363, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Bohumは1304年にヨーロッパで生まれた。彼女はSir Ralph de Bromwichとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1363年に59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Bohum(은)는 1304년 우럽에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Sir Ralph de Bromwich(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1363년 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Bohum nasceu em 1304, em Europa. Ela teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas com Sir Ralph de Bromwich. Ela faleceu em 1363, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Bohum родилась в 1304 г. в местности Европа. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери от Sir Ralph de Bromwich. Она умерла в 1363 г. в возрасте 59 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Bohum nació en 1304, en Europa. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas con Sir Ralph de Bromwich. Murió en 1363, a la edad de 59 años.
